entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

This review has all my changes.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

most of these yaml files are in feature branch for AAI and should have been pushed there and then merged to master.

Patch Set 3:

That is what this review is for...merging the now outdated contents of the feature branch into master.  The feature branch will be deleted after this is in master.

Git "Merges" are not allowed it seems and a rebase is required where the feature branch becomes the new master.  I tried to do this and wasn't sure so this was the approach I took.

Patch Set 3:

thats not the proper way why feature branch was created in the first place? there are multiple commits to the feature branch not merging it with master we will lose track of those patches.

not knowing how to merge feature branch into master is not a good reason for dropping others commits.

Patch Set 3:

I see your point....So what is the proper way to complete a feature branch using gerrit?  Have you done this before?  Any instructions on how to do this?  There is little time left to get this stuff in.  When I tried what I thought was the proper way, there were 40+ commits that happened on master since this branch was cut and they were all rebased onto the branch with conflicts galore.  I was reading a site where they said the final commit back to master was rejected because the rebased commits already existed in the remote master branch.  There are only 5 or so small commits on the feature branch all by the same person.  If they are ok with how we are trying to finish this feature off shouldn't that be enough for this time?  WRT to the feature branch itself, this is all a learning experience for everyone and it was a good experiment to see whether they make life easier or harder.  In this specific case on such a busy master branch it made things harder.

Patch Set 3:

if a feature branch was created for AAI why did we merge any of 1.1 work into master(40+) when there a feature branch was created for this?

I have merged feature branch to master myself I have asked those who have worked in open-source community what you are doing is not proper way. if you don't know how to merge feature branch I would suggest you get help from linux foundation devops team.

This is frustrating that some patches go directly to masters others are told to put in feature branch and it truly meets the definition of double standard.

Patch Set 3:

all the five commits are not by the same person in the feature branch. and they are not small commits. why would you take credit for it when its not your work??? most the patch you pushed to master comes from the seed code from the feature branch.

 
> I see your point....So what is the proper way to complete a feature
 > branch using gerrit?  Have you done this before?  Any instructions
 > on how to do this?  There is little time left to get this stuff in.
 >  When I tried what I thought was the proper way, there were 40+
 > commits that happened on master since this branch was cut and they
 > were all rebased onto the branch with conflicts galore.  I was
 > reading a site where they said the final commit back to master was
 > rejected because the rebased commits already existed in the remote
 > master branch.  There are only 5 or so small commits on the feature
 > branch all by the same person.  If they are ok with how we are
 > trying to finish this feature off shouldn't that be enough for this
 > time?  WRT to the feature branch itself, this is all a learning
 > experience for everyone and it was a good experiment to see whether
 > they make life easier or harder.  In this specific case on such a
 > busy master branch it made things harder.
Uploaded patch set 4: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 4:

Thanks Mandeep and sorry for the rework

Patch Set 4:

All good.  So can I merge this and the related config changes?  Yury and I have some additional changes to get traversal and resources working.

Patch Set 4: -Code-Review

Sure I am good with it being merged
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda
